metadata.title: Domestic Production vs Imports - Critical Industries
metadata.description: Factual, source-backed data on UK domestic production vs import dependency across critical industries, with France and Germany comparisons.
metadata.notes: All values are from official sources. Estimates clearly marked. Import dependency = imports / (production + imports - exports). Self-sufficiency = domestic production / domestic consumption.

industries.name: Energy
industries.definition: Primary energy supply: oil, natural gas, electricity generation. Includes domestic extraction, generation, and net trade.
industries.includes: Crude oil, petroleum products, natural gas, electricity (all sources)
industries.excludes: Refined fuel retail, energy services, energy equipment manufacturing

industries.name: Food & Agriculture
industries.definition: Domestic food production as share of total food consumption. Covers all food categories including crops, livestock, dairy, and processed foods.
industries.includes: Cereals, meat, dairy, eggs, vegetables, fruit, processed foods
industries.excludes: Animal feed imports, non-food agricultural products (cotton, tobacco), beverages

industries.name: Pharmaceuticals
industries.definition: Medicinal and pharmaceutical products trade. Covers finished dosage forms and active pharmaceutical ingredients.
industries.includes: Medicament mixtures in dosage, vaccines, APIs, biological products

industries.name: Steel & Metals
industries.definition: Crude steel production and finished steel product trade. Primary metric is tonnage of crude steel.
industries.includes: Crude steel (oxygen converter + electric arc), finished steel products
industries.excludes: Non-ferrous metals (aluminium, copper), scrap metal trade, downstream fabrication

industries.name: Automotive
industries.definition: Motor vehicle manufacturing, including passenger cars and commercial vehicles. Trade measured by value and units.
industries.includes: Passenger cars, commercial vehicles, automotive parts
industries.excludes: Motorcycles, bicycles, rail vehicles, aftermarket accessories

industries.name: Semiconductors & Electronics
industries.definition: Semiconductor devices and integrated circuits. Covers chip design, fabrication, and trade.
industries.includes: Integrated circuits, discrete semiconductors, memory chips, processors
industries.excludes: Consumer electronics (phones, TVs), software, PCB assembly

industries.name: Defence & Aerospace
industries.definition: Military equipment and civil aerospace manufacturing and trade. Includes aircraft, weapons systems, and space systems.
industries.includes: Military aircraft, naval vessels, armoured vehicles, missiles, civil aircraft, aero engines, satellites
industries.excludes: Dual-use IT systems, private security services, veteran services

industries.name: Construction Materials
industries.definition: Key construction inputs: timber, cement, and aggregates. Measured by volume and import share of consumption.
industries.includes: Sawn timber, cement/clinker, sand, gravel, crushed stone, bricks
industries.excludes: Steel for construction (covered under Steel), glass, insulation, fixtures

sources.name: DESNZ Digest of UK Energy Statistics (DUKES)
sources.provider: Department for Energy Security & Net Zero
sources.covers: UK energy production, consumption, imports, exports by fuel type
sources.limitations: 12-18 month lag on final data; provisional estimates released quarterly

sources.name: DEFRA Food Statistics Pocketbook / UK Food Security Index
sources.provider: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs
sources.covers: UK food self-sufficiency ratios by product, food trade balances
sources.limitations: Self-sufficiency calculated on production basis, not calorie basis; excludes feed imports

sources.name: HMRC UK Trade Info
sources.covers: Detailed bilateral trade by commodity (HS codes), value and volume
sources.limitations: Post-Brexit methodology changes; asymmetries with EU partner data

sources.name: ONS UK Trade
sources.covers: Balance of payments, trade in goods by commodity
sources.limitations: Revisions common; services trade estimates have wider confidence intervals

sources.name: Eurostat
sources.covers: Energy dependency, trade, agriculture, industrial production across EU
sources.limitations: UK data ends 2020 for EU membership series; post-Brexit treated as third country

sources.name: OECD Statistics
sources.provider: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
sources.covers: Bilateral trade, industrial production, input-output tables
sources.limitations: 2-year lag on some datasets; aggregation may obscure detail

sources.name: World Steel Association
sources.covers: Crude steel production by country, apparent steel use
sources.limitations: Crude steel only; does not cover downstream products

sources.name: SMMT Motor Industry Facts
sources.provider: Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders
sources.covers: UK vehicle production, registrations, trade values
sources.limitations: Value data includes parts trade; unit data covers complete vehicles only

current.notes: Oil and gas production declining. Norway supplies ~57% of gas imports.
Electricity near self-sufficient due to nuclear fleet. Oil and gas almost entirely imported.
Ended domestic hard coal production 2018. Highest energy dependency of the three.
65% is estimated for 2024. Fresh vegetables at record low since 1988.
France is the EU's largest agricultural producer. 129% on calorie basis.
Self-sufficient in staples but heavily import-dependent for fresh produce.
Trade near-balanced but shifted from surplus to deficit since 2015. API dependency on China/India.
Europe's largest pharma exporter. Strong surplus despite API import dependency.
Near-balanced trade. Significant pharma sector but growing import dependency.
Production at historic low. 70% import dependency up from 55% in 2022.
Europe's largest producer. Net exporter. Consumption at mid-1990s low.
Moderate producer. Lower electricity costs support domestic production.
UK produces mostly for export (80%). Domestic market heavily import-dependent.
4.6x UK production. Dominant European auto exporter. Top destinations: US, UK, France.
1.7x UK production. Renault/Stellantis. Exports declined 8.1% in 2024.
Minimal domestic fabrication. Almost entirely import-dependent. Govt pledged $1.2bn investment.
Revenue down 33% since 2019. EU Chips Act aims to address dependency.
Small domestic market. STMicroelectronics only significant producer.
Strong net exporter. Aerospace is 56% of defence exports. BAE Systems, Rolls-Royce, Leonardo UK.
2nd best year for defence exports. Dassault Rafale backlog at record. Airbus major contributor.
Record exports. Ukraine = 64% of 2024 defence exports. Rheinmetall major supplier.
World's 3rd largest net timber importer. Cement at record import share. Aggregates domestic.
Large forest base. Timber net exporter. Cement net importer (France largest source).
Net timber importer despite domestic harvest. Significant wood trade deficit.

importDependency.description: Import dependency ratio by industry (%, latest available year)

domesticShare.description: Domestic production share of consumption (%, latest available year)

foodBreakdown.description: UK food self-sufficiency by product category (%, 2023)

constructionBreakdown.description: UK construction materials import dependency by material (%, 2023)
